resources.string: The OBB is injected at first launch
Done. Launch the game from your home screen — the first launch will take a few seconds while the OBB unpacks.
\n→ Uninstall any existing version of the game first.
Allow installing unknown apps for OBB Installer.

The OBB is bundled inside the APK. A tiny invisible component injected next to the game extracts it on first launch — directly from inside the game\'s process, where every Android version allows writing to the OBB folder. No special permissions, no OEM can block this.
The game is re-signed with the hub\'s key, so Play Store updates are no longer possible and any online anti-cheat will reject it. Single-player and offline games run normally.
A standalone installer for Android games that ship as APK + OBB.
Installs the APK with one tap.
Embeds the OBB inside the APK so the game finds it on its own.
Auto-bumps targetSdk on legacy games so modern Android accepts them.
Auto-patches old native libraries with text relocations (libogg, libcrypto, …).
Android 11+ (Xiaomi/HyperOS, OnePlus, Samsung One UI, Pixel, etc.). 90 %+ of single-player legacy games install and run.
Where do I get the APK and OBB?
From your existing backup, or from a sideload archive. The hub does not download anything.

A small fraction of legacy games verify their own signature in code and refuse to run after re-signing. Most online competitive games will also reject the patched signature. There is no fix for those at the hub level.
First launch is slow / black screen.
Normal. The injected component is unpacking the OBB into the game\'s private folder. ~30 s per gigabyte. Subsequent launches are instant.
Will it update from Play Store?
No. Re-signed packages cannot be updated from Play Store. Reinstall through the hub when a new version is available.
